diffCoverage.notes: Reviewed the credential-at-rest cipher, the SQLite store, redaction, and the command/IPC boundary for secret leakage and injection.

findings.evidence: The keychain seal/open and ciphertext-on-disk invariants are proven only by fixed-key unit tests (crypto.rs). The real-OS-keychain roundtrip (cred_store_roundtrip), QR scan (qr_login_live), and relaunch auto-restore are #[ignore] and have not been run.
findings.fix: Run `cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml -- --ignored cred_store_roundtrip --nocapture` and verify relaunch auto-restore on the GUI; capture the results in the evidence bundle before passes:true.

findings.evidence: Message bodies are stored in SQLite as plaintext. These are user data on the user's own machine (not a bearer token), and ADR-0005 accepts this for the durability goal; whole-DB encryption is deferred.
findings.fix: Track whole-DB/at-rest encryption (SQLCipher) as a future ADR; record as an accepted known risk in the evidence bundle.

findings.evidence: Raw capture is redacted by default; only the loudly-warned ZCA_LOG_RAW=1 opt-in writes unredacted payloads, intended for local debugging. The single capture_raw call site logs cookie names + counts only, not values.
findings.fix: Keep ZCA_LOG_RAW off in any shared/CI environment; no code change required.

summary: Security review of the encrypted credential store + QR/persistence/logging changes. Code-level security is sound: credentials are AES-256-GCM sealed (random 12-byte nonce, 32-byte key) under a keychain-held master key and stored as ciphertext (asserted by a default unit test); AES-GCM errors are opaque and a truncated blob is rejected without panic; the keychain holds only the master key. redact.rs masks every real Zalo secret cookie name (zpw_sek/zpsid/zpw_enk/imei/cookie/secret/token/session) and raw capture is redacted by default. All SQL is positional-parameter bound (no injection). No command returns or accepts credential/token values; the token-bearing GotLoginInfo QR event is dropped before the webview (test-asserted); restore_sessions/load_history return only non-secret/user-data DTOs. Decision is needs-human, not pass, because the live smoke proofs (real keychain roundtrip + relaunch auto-restore) are #[ignore] and unrun. No blocking findings.
